title: Hide admin links from client portal, chat online/offline toggle, remove watermark, add client profile with support tier

requirements.summary: Remove all admin panel links and references from the client portal UI
acceptanceCriteria: No links or buttons pointing to /master or /staff appear anywhere in the client portal after login.
The client portal header, footer, tabs, and dashboard contain zero references to admin or staff routes.
Navigating directly to /master and /staff still works as before.

file_operations.description: Review and remove any links, buttons, or references to /master or /staff routes from the client portal dashboard and navigation tabs.
Ensure the Header component does not display any navigation links to admin or staff panels when the current user role is 'client'.

requirements.summary: Add chat availability toggle in admin panels with offline mode for client portal
acceptanceCriteria: Master panel and Staff panel each have a clearly labeled toggle or button to set chat status to Online or Offline.
Chat status is stored in localStorage (e.g., key 'chat_status') and defaults to Online.
When chat status is Offline, the client portal chat tab shows a neon-styled 'Support chat is currently offline' message and the message input is hidden or disabled.
When chat status is Online, the client portal chat works as normal.
Changes to the toggle are reflected immediately without requiring a page reload.

file_operations.description: Add a chat availability toggle (Online/Offline) using a Button or Switch component at the top of the Chat section. Store the chat status in localStorage under the key 'chat_status' (default 'Online'). Update the state immediately when toggled.

Add a chat availability toggle (Online/Offline) using a Button or Switch component at the top of the Chat section. Read and write to localStorage key 'chat_status'. Ensure the toggle state reflects the current localStorage value on mount.

Read the chat status from localStorage ('chat_status'). If the status is 'Offline', display a neon-styled message 'Support chat is currently offline' and hide or disable the message input field. If 'Online', render the chat interface normally.
Ensure the Chat tab in the client portal passes the necessary props to ChatView and respects the chat availability status for UI updates.

requirements.summary: Remove the 'Build with Caffeine AI' watermark from all pages
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Build with Caffeine AI', 'Powered by Caffeine', or similar text appears on any page or component.
No hidden or visually subtle watermark referencing Caffeine AI exists in the DOM.

file_operations.description: Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text or footer elements from the ClientPortal component.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text or footer elements from the MasterPanel component.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text or footer elements from the StaffPanel component.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text from the ClientLogin page.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text from the MasterLogin page.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text from the StaffLogin page.
Search for and remove any 'Build with Caffeine AI' or similar attribution text from the Header component.

requirements.summary: Add support tier field to client records with management in Master panel
acceptanceCriteria: The Client TypeScript interface and db.ts include a 'tier' field typed as 'Basic' | 'Premium' | 'Enterprise'.
Newly created clients default to 'Basic' tier.
The Master admin panel clients table displays each client's current tier.
Master admin can change any client's tier via a dropdown (Basic / Premium / Enterprise); the change is immediately persisted to localStorage.
Tier changes are reflected instantly without a full page reload.

file_operations.description: Add a 'tier' field to the Client interface with type 'Basic' | 'Premium' | 'Enterprise'. Make it optional with a default type or union including undefined for backward compatibility.

Update the createClient function to set tier to 'Basic' by default when creating new clients. Add a new function updateClientTier(luidId: string, tier: 'Basic' | 'Premium' | 'Enterprise') that updates the tier field in localStorage and returns the updated client.

Add a 'Support Tier' column to the clients table. For each client row, display the current tier (defaulting to 'Basic' if undefined) and include a Select dropdown with options Basic, Premium, Enterprise. On change, call updateClientTier from db.ts and refresh the table state immediately.
Ensure newly created clients via AddClientForm default to 'Basic' tier by passing the default value when calling createClient.

requirements.summary: Add client profile section displaying LUID ID, creation date, and support tier badge
acceptanceCriteria: The client portal header or dashboard contains a clickable profile element showing the user's LUID ID.
Clicking the profile element opens a modal or inline panel with: LUID ID, account creation date, and support tier.
The support tier is displayed as a styled badge using the neon green theme (e.g., green glow for Premium/Enterprise, muted for Basic).
The tier displayed matches the value stored in localStorage for that client.
If the master admin changes the tier, refreshing the portal shows the updated tier.
The profile view can be dismissed or closed.

file_operations.description: Create a ClientProfile component that accepts luidId as a prop. Fetch the client record from localStorage using getClient(luidId). Display the LUID ID, account creation date (formatted), and support tier as a styled badge. Use a neon green glow for Premium/Enterprise tiers and a muted style for Basic. Render this inside a Dialog or Modal component from shadcn/ui for dismissibility.
For users with role 'client', add a clickable profile icon or text element displaying the user's LUID ID in the header. Wire this element to open the ClientProfile modal/dialog when clicked.

Import and integrate the ClientProfile component. Add state to control the profile modal open/closed state. Pass the profile toggle handler to Header or render the ClientProfile dialog conditionally based on the open state.
